content.full_text:
Law Offices

Michael Schaefer
Admitted to all courts
of California & Nevada, the
United States Supreme Court
and United States Tax Court

SAN DIEGO OFFICE:
LA JOLLA CORPORATE CENTER
3252 Holiday Court, Suite 103
La Jolla, California 92037
Phone & Fax (619) 558-4750

LOS ANGELES OFFICE:
255 S. Grand Avenue, Suite 2006
Los Angeles, California 90012
Phone & Fax (213) 687-4638

February 19, 1991

Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J St. #800
PO Box 807
Sacramento, Cal. 95804-0807

Re: City Council District 9
     City of Los Angeles

I am a candidate in the above election, and do not
yet have a campaign I.D. No. for purposes of filing
reports with your office and with the City Clerk
Elections Division.

If you have sent it out in recent days, it will
cross in the mails with this letter, and I'll get it.

On another issue, I had contemplated sending out
some 30,000 homes housing 58,000 voters
offering 1/4 hour consultation, as an attorney, without
charge, as to any legal problem the constituent might have,
after my election, with the matter to be resolved by a
recommendation and referral to L.A. county bar referral
attorneys if it wasn't something a councilman could handle
administratively involving the City. Being salaried by the
City, I would no longer charge legal fees. Would require
only that the citizen be a registered voter in the district
and have proof of voting(for whomever, total discretion
of the voter; this limits the data-base to 12,000 instead of
58,000, turnout being maybe 20% in city elections). But this
appears to violate prohibitions against influencing voters
directly or indirectly, to vote, or agree to vote, Elections
Code sec. 12003. And I'd be influencing and encouraging voters
wanted me there to be able to assist), so have concluded this
would be an unlawful practice, a crime jeopardizing the entire
election. If you disagree please advise.

[signature: M Schaefer]

MICHAEL SCHAEFER
